description: Implement FGAP-126's corrected resolution: (1) apply the FGAP-068 caller-as-reconciler shape so a non-interactive run-work-order-loop dispatch never needs to invoke the 8 pi-only gated tools (author-agent-spec, author-tool-grant, commit-attested, workflow-execute, workflow-resume, workflow-init, monitors-control, monitors-rules) itself -- the interactive orchestrator performs any of these when needed, not the headless dispatch; (2) route run-work-order-loop's own commit through the gated commit-attested tool path in place of the current ungated _internals.attestedCommit library call (work-order-loop.ts:254), closing the gate-coverage inconsistency where a passing non-interactive run could already produce an attested commit with no operator confirm while the less consequential spec-authoring is refused outright.
acceptance_criteria: A non-interactive run-work-order-loop dispatch does not invoke, and is not refused on, any of the 8 pi-only gated tools -- verified by runtime demo, per the caller-as-reconciler shape (the interactive orchestrator performs these when needed, not the headless dispatch).

run-work-order-loop's commit is produced via the gated commit-attested tool path, not the current ungated library call at work-order-loop.ts:254 -- verified by runtime demo that the same authorization commit-attested enforces for every other caller now applies here.

npm run build, npm run check, npm test all pass clean; docs-surface-sync applied to any changed op/tool description (ops-registry.ts, package README, skill-narrative.md) per this project's binding convention.
